mechanism_narrative: Industrial Production index growth reflects expanding supply-side capacity utilization, which correlates with positive aggregate earnings momentum and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns do not positively correlate with INDPRO momentum (Spearman p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points in three months signals deteriorating labor income and weakening consumer demand, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and causes negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances when UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months, or the signal's Rank IC is negative over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in the federal funds rate and consumer price index amplify the real policy tightening burden, compressing equity risk premiums and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL both rise over the prior 3 months
failure_condition: The joint effect of FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL yields a Rank IC below 0.04 over any 2-year window, or the interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in a pooled regression.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth accelerates corporate revenue expansion during post-recession recovery periods, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns do not exceed 60% hit rate in post-recession recovery quarters, or GDPC1 growth has zero correlation with forward returns during recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Growth in nonfarm payrolls increases household income and consumer spending, which translates into higher aggregate demand and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are not positive in > 55% of months with above-median PAYEMS growth, or Rank IC < 0.03 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in industrial production and rising unemployment constitutes a joint recessionary signal that depresses earnings expectations and risk appetite, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines over 3 months and UNRATE rises > 0.2pp over same period
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce negative 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction term's t-statistic falls below 1.5 in a walk-forward regression.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in housing starts signals contraction in residential investment and related durable goods demand, which produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls > 100 thousand units over 2 months
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns are positive in > 50% of instances when HOUST falls > 100k units in 2 months, or the signal's Rank IC is positive over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth reflects robust consumer demand, supporting corporate top-line growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are not positively correlated with RSAFS momentum (Spearman p > 0.05), or the signal's hit rate is below 55% over any 2-year window.
